title: Lithocarpus castellarnauianus
intro: Lithocarpus castellarnauianus, also known as Castellarnau's Lithocarpus or Castanea castellarnauiana, is a species of tree in the family Fagaceae, native to the Philippines and closely related to the chestnut family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus castellarnauianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lithocarpus castellarnauianus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the formulation of capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus castellarnauianus is a species of tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of trees commonly known as Lithocarpus or stone oaks. The species is native to the island of Mindanao in the Philippines. Lithocarpus castellarnauianus is classified as an evergreen tree, with a broad, rounded crown and a straight, columnar trunk. The tree is a part of the broad and diverse family of Fagaceae, which includes over 600 species of trees and shrubs.
